bullets.text: Russian attacks on merchant ships in the Black Sea have intensified, killing ten people, with 28 deaths reported in the Odesa region in July alone.
Seven American aid workers are quarantining at an Ebola facility in Kenya after returning from Congo, following a US travel ban related to the outbreak.
Mexican drug cartel leader Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada, co-founder of the Sinaloa Cartel, has been sentenced to life in a US prison and ordered to pay $15bn for drug trafficking.
The Northern Territory Parliament is preparing to debate contentious child protection laws, which have drawn criticism for their potential impact on the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle.
An alleged 'ISIS bride' has been granted bail in Melbourne after facing terror charges.
One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has shared a Seven Network pitch for a 'Spotlight' special, which she says would highlight 'uncontrolled immigration' in Australia.
The new UK Prime Minister, Andy Burnham, has unsettled investors on his first day in office, causing government bonds to plunge due to concerns about his fiscal approach.

Kevin Warsh, the new US central bank chief, is reportedly reluctant to signal future interest rate moves, leading to uncertainty in how hedge funds will react and how the Federal Reserve will manage its balance sheet.
Netflix plans to raise $1 billion through a US high-grade bond sale, its first in two years, as slowing sales growth brings increased scrutiny on the company's financial future.
